140 
chapter 6
making the DotComBust
Let’5 build the REAL game: 
“Sink a Dot Com”
We’ve been working on the ‘simple’ version, but now 
let’5 build the real one. Instead of a single row, we’11 
use a grid. And instead of one DotCom, we’11 use 
three.
Goal: Sink all of the computer’5 Dot Coms in the 
fewest number of guesses. You’re given a rating level 
based on how well you perform.
Setup: When the game program is launched, the 
computer places three Dot Coms, randomly, on the 
virtual 7 x 7 grid. When that’5 complete, the game 
asks for your first guess.
How you play: We haven’t learned to build a GUI 
yet, 50 this version works at the command-line. The 
computer will prompt you to enter a guess (a cell), 
which you’11 type at the command-line (as “A3”, “C5”, 
etc.). In response to your guess, you’11 see a result at 
the command-line, either “hit”, “miss”, or “You sunk 
Pets.com” (or whatever the lucky Dot Com of the day 
is). When you’ve sent all three Dot Coms to that big 
404 in the sky, the game ends by printing out your 
rating.
7 X 7 grid 
File Edit Window Help Sell
